titleStmt.title: SII 9.167: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 167. (A.R. No. 673 of 1922.) ON A BROKEN SLAB LYING IN THE SAMBHULINGESVARA TEMPLE AT SIRUGUPPA, SAME TALUK AND DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is damaged and dated Chālukya-Vikrama year 27, Chitrabhānu, Pushya, śu. 6, Budhavāra, corresponding to A.D. 1102 December 17, Wednesday. It refers itself to the reign of the Chāḷukya king Tribhuvanamalladēva, whose feudatory Tribhuvanamalla-Pāṇḍyarasa was ruling Noḷambavāḍi thirtytwo-thousand. Chokkarāya, Bīvarasa, Lōkarasa and others are said to have made a grant of some land for the service of some god (name lost).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . samasta bhuvanāśraya
hārājādhirāja paramēśvara paramabhaṭṭāraka
śrayakuḷatiḷakacāḷukyābharaṇa śrī
mattribhuvanamalladēvara vijayarājyamuttarōtta
rābhivṛddhipravarddhamānamācaṁdrārkatāraṁbaraṁ salutta
ballakuṁdeya nelevīḍinoḷ rājyaṁgeyyuttamire
padmōpajīvi samadhigatapaṁcamahāśabda mahāmaṇḍaḷēśvara kāṁ
cīpuravarēsvaraṁ yaduvaṁśābaradyumaṇi samya
cūḍāmaṇi nijakuḷakamaḷamārttaṇḍa
digaṇḍa rājigacōḷamanōbhaṁgaṁ śrīmatṛbhavanamalla
pādābjabhriṁgaṁ śrīmanmahāmaṇḍaḷēśvaraṁ tṛbhuvanamallapāṁ
ḍyarasaru kuttuṁbittibhattagrāme
vāḍi mūvaticchāsiramumaṁ
duṣṭanigrahasiṣṭapratipāḷanadiṁ rājyaṁgeyyuttire
nāyakaṁ cūkkarāyaṁ cāḷukyavikrama
27 neya citrabhānusaṁvatsarada puṣya suddha 6 budhavāra
yaṇasaṁkrāṁtiya parvvanimittadiṁ ballakuṁde
roḷagaṇa bhattagrāme
ppeya svayaṁbhudēva dhūpa
nivēdyakkaṁ dēvālyada khaṁḍasphuṭitajīrṇōddhārakkaṁ
sirivurada ṁ keya
kōḍiyalu biṭṭa gadde mattaroṁdu vada
biṭṭa moge mattaroṁdu ballakuṁdeya
ṟa manneyaṁ bīvarasanuṁ
lōkarasanu tekkekallacokkarasa
leya jaggarasanu samastaprajegā
vuṁḍagaḷumiḻduballakuṁde mū
nūṟaṟalu mūraloṁdu paṇamaṁ
koṭṭarintī dharmmavanāvanōrvvaṁ pratipā
ḷisidaṁ kurukṣētra vāraṇāsi prayā
geyalu sāsira kavileyaṁ sa
hasra brāhmargge dānaṁgeyda
phaḷamakku .
Digital edition of SII 9.167 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
